perturbation.original_text: LICENSOR warrants that Content does not infringe an intellectual property right enforceable in the agreed country of delivery or use. LICENSOR indemnifies and holds harmless plan_b against all claims that Content infringes any of the above mentioned rights of a third Party. LICENSOR shall pay all damages awarded in a trial to a third Party.
perturbation.changed_text: LICENSOR warrants that Content does not infringe an intellectual property right enforceable in the agreed country of delivery or use. LICENSOR indemnifies and holds harmless plan_b against all claims that Content infringes any of the above mentioned rights of a third Party. LICENSOR's liability for damages awarded in a trial to a third party is limited to the total revenues received by LICENSOR under this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction in Section 5.4. Initially, the LICENSOR was obligated to pay all damages awarded in a trial to a third party. The modification limits this liability to the total revenues received under the agreement. This introduces uncertainty about the extent of LICENSOR's responsibility for intellectual property infringement, contradicting the previous unlimited obligation. This affects the risk assessment of plan_b.
perturbation.contradicted_text: LICENSOR warrants that Content does not infringe an intellectual property right enforceable in the agreed country of delivery or use. LICENSOR indemnifies and holds harmless plan_b against all claims that Content infringes any of the above mentioned rights of a third Party. LICENSOR's liability for damages awarded in a trial to a third party is limited to the total revenues received by LICENSOR under this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that LICENSOR 'shall pay all damages awarded in a trial to a third Party,' implying unlimited liability. The changed text limits LICENSOR's liability to 'the total revenues received by LICENSOR under this agreement.' This directly contradicts the original unlimited obligation, creating a clear conflict in terms of financial responsibility. Because the Changed text is also the Contradicted Text in this instance, it further causes a contradiction within the clause. The Perturbation Explanation further elaborates the contradiction of the conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Unless otherwise stated in the Appendix the term of this letter Agreement shall continue for twenty-four (24) months with the effective date unless terminated sooner or extended pursuant to the terms hereof ("Initial Term"). The Initial Term shall automatically be extended for an additional period of half a year unless either party provides the other party with written notification of termination of the letter Agreement at least 60 days prior to end of such period.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless otherwise stated in the Appendix the term of this letter Agreement shall continue for twenty-four (24) months with the effective date unless terminated sooner or extended pursuant to the terms hereof ("Initial Term"). The Initial Term may be extended for an additional period of half a year only if both parties mutually agree to extend the term in writing at least 60 days prior to the end of such period.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that the contract is automatically extended for six months unless one party gives notice of termination. The modified clause states that the contract may be extended only if both parties agree. This contradiction creates uncertainty about the renewal process. The original version implied automatic renewal unless cancelled, while the modified version requires active agreement. The term can be interpreted differently
perturbation.contradicted_text: Unless otherwise stated in the Appendix the term of this letter Agreement shall continue for twenty-four (24) months with the effective date unless terminated sooner or extended pursuant to the terms hereof ("Initial Term"). The Initial Term may be extended for an additional period of half a year only if both parties mutually agree to extend the term in writing at least 60 days prior to the end of such period.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces the automatic renewal clause with a clause requiring mutual agreement for extension. This directly contradicts the original automatic renewal provision, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The original version states the 'Initial Term shall automatically be extended', while the modified version states the 'Initial Term may be extended only if both parties mutually agree'. This creates conflicting obligations and uncertainty regarding the renewal process.
